workflow.name: Extract invoice data from Gmail to Airtable with Mistral OCR and GPT-4.1-mini
workflow.description:
This n8n template demonstrates how to automatically process invoice attachments from email using OCR and AI. When an invoice is received in Gmail, the workflow extracts structured invoice data and stores it in Airtable while preventing duplicates.

This automation is useful for freelancers, agencies, and finance teams who receive invoices by email and want to maintain a structured invoice database without manual data entry.

## Who’s it for

This workflow is designed for:

Freelancers managing client invoices

Agencies handling multiple invoice emails

Finance teams automating invoice intake

Automation consultants building accounting workflows

## How it works

When a new email with an attachment arrives in Gmail, the workflow checks whether the subject contains the word “invoice”. The attachment is uploaded to an image-hosting service to generate a public URL.

The invoice file is then processed using OCR to extract text content. An AI agent analyzes the extracted text and converts it into structured invoice fields such as invoice number, sender, recipient, dates, description, and amount. 

Before saving, Airtable is searched to ensure the invoice does not already exist. Valid invoices are stored automatically, while invalid data triggers an error notification email.

## How to set up

- Connect Gmail credentials and enable attachment download.

- Connect Airtable credentials and create an invoice table.

- Add your imgbb API key in the HTTP Request node.

- Connect your OCR provider (Mistral).

- Connect your OpenAI API credentials.

- Activate the workflow and send a test invoice email.

## Requirements

- Gmail account

- Airtable account

- OpenAI API key

- Mistral OCR API key

- imgbb API key

- n8n instance (cloud or self-hosted)

## How to customize the workflow

You can customize this workflow by:

Modifying the AI prompt used for invoice extraction

Adding new invoice fields in Airtable

Changing validation rules

Supporting additional invoice formats (PDF, PNG, JPG)

Adding integrations with accounting tools like QuickBooks or Stripe

workflow.name: Automated invoice data extraction & tracking workflow

Here is an invoice that you should analyze: {{ $json.pages[0].markdown }}

parameters.subject: =⚠️ Invoice Processing Error - {{ $json.step }}

parameters.content:
# 🛠️ Setup Guide

**Author:** [Safa Khan]()

Follow the steps below to get this workflow up and running:

### ✅ Step 1: Choose Your Trigger

Determine what will kick off this workflow. In this example, we’re using Gmail as the trigger node to watch for incoming emails with invoice attachments.

### ✅ Step 2: Connect Gmail Credentials

Make sure your Gmail OAuth credentials are connected and attachment downloading is enabled in the trigger node settings.

### ✅ Step 3: Connect Airtable

Connect your Airtable credentials and confirm your invoice database is ready.

Airtable base link: https://airtable.com/appU9XzFqL5Aovj2M/shrnanmtZpUgbVsEo

### ✅ Step 4: Add Image Hosting API

#### https://api.imgbb.com/

Connect your imgbb API key in the HTTP Request node.
This step uploads invoice attachments and generates a public image URL required for OCR processing.

### ✅ Step 5: Connect OCR + AI Model

#### https://admin.mistral.ai/organization/api-keys

Connect your AI services to power the extraction and structuring steps:

OCR provider (for text extraction)

AI model (for structured invoice data)

These nodes convert invoice images into usable invoice records automatically.

### 💡 Final Step: Test It Out

Send a test email with an invoice attachment and verify:

Attachment uploads correctly

OCR extracts text

AI structures invoice data

Airtable record is created

Duplicate invoices are prevented

user.name: Safa Khan
user.bio:
Automation specialist helping businesses eliminate manual work using n8n and AI-powered workflows.
I design custom automations that connect tools like Google Calendar, Airtable, GPT, Supabase, and internal systems turning scattered processes into streamlined operations.
From invoice processing to CRM automation and scheduling systems, I build scalable solutions that save time, reduce errors, and increase efficiency.
Helping SMBs work smarter without needing a full tech team.

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
